expectations.text: Ran doc_structure_analyzer.py and referenced its structured output
expectations.evidence: Did not run doc_structure_analyzer.py. All findings came from manual README.md reads with line numbers.

expectations.text: Named specific README sections with their suggested docs/ destination
expectations.evidence: Named 6 specific sections with line numbers: Hooks Reference (114-141)→docs/hooks.md, Policy System (66-87)→new docs/policies.md, MCP Servers (143-158)→docs/mcp.md, CLI Commands (89-111)→XTRM-GUIDE.md, Version History (179-188)→remove, Plugin Structure (52-63)→borderline.

expectations.text: Report is actionable — tells user exactly what to do next, not just observations
expectations.evidence: Each section has a specific Recommendation: block with exact action (Remove section, Add single link, Create docs/policies.md, etc.). Estimated README would shrink from 193 to 60-70 lines.

expectations.text: Did not edit or create any files (audit only)
expectations.evidence: Report explicitly states no files were modified. Audit-only as instructed.

notes: Strong manual analysis — named sections with precise line numbers, more detail than the script-based approach for pure audit. The weakness is no structured/reproducible output and missed the --fix context from the skill. Comparable quality to with_skill on this task.
